title: althenia filiformis subsp. barrandonii
intro: Althenia filiformis subsp. barrandonii, also known as Pyrenean althenia, is a rare, subalpine plant species native to the Pyrenees mountains. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The health benefits of Pyrenean althenia include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its availability and usage may be limited due to its rarity and limited scientific research.
